reforms.description: Slovakia's start-up incentives consist of one-off subsidies for unemployed individuals starting a business, with spending on these measures increasing in recent years. However, the effectiveness of these programs is variable, with evidence suggesting low net positive impacts on employment due to high deadweight losses and lack of targeted monitoring.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the incentives aim to foster entrepreneurship, their lack of targeting and monitoring may lead to inefficiencies and limited impact on actual job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The start-up incentives have been allocated and spending has increased.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant allocation of resources towards supporting entrepreneurship, although its effectiveness is questioned due to implementation issues.
reforms.source_quote: Start-up incentives consist in one-off subsidies allocated to the unemployed starting a business and are covered by two programmes in Slovakia.

reforms.description: The Slovak government approved the Lifelong Learning and Lifelong Guidance Strategy in 2007, establishing a regulatory framework for continuing education and the recognition of non-formal education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing lifelong learning opportunities is expected to improve the skill set of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and adaptability in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act on lifelong learning adopted in 2009 set a new regulatory framework.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to education and training, aiming to enhance the skills of the workforce and address labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The Act on lifelong learning adopted in 2009 set a new regulatory framework for continuing education, for the accreditation and the assessment of training programmes.

reforms.description: The Slovak government established the Cabinet Council for Innovation in 2011 to ensure better coordination of innovation policy across government bodies and to evaluate innovation policy regularly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination of innovation policy is expected to enhance the effectiveness of public spending on R&D and innovation, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Cabinet Council for Innovation was established to coordinate innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated council for innovation represents a significant step towards improving the governance of innovation policy, which can lead to better outcomes in R&D and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Co-ordination of innovation policy is ensured by the Cabinet Council for Innovation established in 2011.

reforms.description: In 2011, the Slovak government launched the JEREMIE initiative, an EU programme aimed at providing funding to innovative SMEs, complementing R&D financial support with extended tax credits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding and tax incentives to innovative SMEs, the initiative is expected to stimulate private sector investment in R&D and innovation, enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched the JEREMIE initiative to provide funding to innovative SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The JEREMIE initiative represents a significant effort to enhance funding for innovative SMEs, which is crucial for fostering entrepreneurship and innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched in 2011 the JEREMIE initiative, an EU programme providing funding to innovative SMEs.

reforms.description: Slovakia's public investment in research and development is low by OECD standards, and there is a call for greater public resources to support the R&D sector, which is crucial for enhancing the knowledge economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public investment in R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and economic growth by improving the knowledge economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for greater public resources for R&D has been highlighted in strategic documents.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical gap in public investment in R&D, which is essential for enhancing Slovakia's competitiveness and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The research and development sector demands greater public resources rather than restrictions due to the consolidation process.
